properties.remark: ADOBE FENCE BLOW OVER DURING STRONG WINDS SATURDAY EVENING. WIND GUSTS ESTIMATED AT OVER 60 MPH.
MESONET STATION AIB, HOPKINS FIELD AP, NUCLA.
ASOS STATION CAG, CRAIG-MOFFAT COUNTY AIRPORT.
ROCK AND DEBRIS FLOW REPORTED NEAR IRON HORSE FROM NWS EMPLOYEE.
MINOR MUD SLIDE CLOSED WEST BOUND LANE OF INTERSTATE 70.
CONFIRMED TORNADO WITH ROTATION ON THE GROUND AT ABOUT 8500 FEET FOR ABOUT ONE MINUTE. FUNNEL CLOUD LASTED FOR AT LEAST TEN MINUTES.

STRONG WINDS FROM THUNDERSTORMS KNOCKED DOWN POWER LINES CAUSING WIDESPREAD POWER OUTAGES ON JULY 10TH AROUND 4:40 PM FROM WEST OF STEAMBOAT TOWARDS HAYDEN. WIND GUSTS WERE REPORTED BETWEEN 40 AND 50 MPH WITH THE STORMS. LIGHTNING ALSO STRUCK A HOUSE. UNSURE IF THESE WERE CAUSED BY DOWNED TREES OR NOT.
MUDSLIDES AND DEBRIS FLOWS ALONG HIGHWAY 145 BETWEEN MILE MARKERS 76 AND 77 DUE TO HEAVY RAIN.
POWER OUT IN DELTA, CEDAR EDGE, AND THE PEACH VALLEY AREA WITH TREES DOWN ALONG HWY 65.
SOME DEBRIS OVER SR 162 BUT TRAFFIC WAS NOT IMPACTED.
DEBRIS FLOW ACROSS HIGHWAY 261 ON THE WAY TO KANE GULCH. DRIVER FOUND AT LEAST 4 INCHES OF MUD AND SILT BLOCKING THE ROAD AND COULD NOT PASS ACROSS.
MUDSLIDE REPORTED ACROSS HWY 145 ALONG CR 58P, NEAR MILEMARKER 75.
HIGHWAY 145 BETWEEN MILE MARKERS 77 AND 80 CLOSED DUE TO MUDSLIDES FROM HEAVY RAIN.
GAL THAT DROVE TO WORK AT THE ANGLER INN FROM NORWOOD SAID HWY 145 HAD SOME MUDSLIDES ON IT.
FLASH FLOODING WAS REPORTED ALONG ONE OF THE WASHES RUNNING ALONG DELICATE ARCH ROAD IN SIDE THE PARK.
REPORT OF ROCKS ON ROAD NEAR MM 123 IN HWY 160. IT WAS CLEANED UP BY LOCAL POLICE.
RUBBERMAID STORAGE CABINET IN THE GARAGE FELL OVER AND BOTH CABINET DOORS FLEW OFF. REPORT VIA SOCIAL MEDIA.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS NON-TSTM WND GST REPORT FROM EAGLE COUNTY RGNL AIRPORT. AWOS STATION EGE, EAGLE COUNTY RGNL AIRPORT.

MUDSLIDE ON HIGHWAY 160 AT MM124. CARS WERE STUCK IN DEEP MUD WITH TRAFFIC BACKED UP ON EITHER SIDE OF HWY 160 WITH ABOUT 100 CARS ON EITHER SIDE. NO MEASUREMENT OR ESTIMATE OF HOW DEEP THE MUD WAS. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
SEGMENT OF WOODEN FENCE WAS KNOCKED DOWN DUE TO WINDS. REPORT VIA SOCIAL MEDIA.
DAMAGE TO ROOF SHINGLES.
BLOWING DUST WITH VISIBILITY LESS THAN A QUARTER MILE.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS NON-TSTM WND GST REPORT FROM GRAND JUNCTION RGNL AP. ASOS STATION GJT, GRAND JUNCTION RGNL AP.
MUD AND DEBRIS ON HIGHWAY 151 AT MM 28 DUE TO HEAVY RAIN FROM THUNDERSTORMS EARLIER.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS DEBRIS FLOW REPORT FROM 5 W CHIMNEY ROCK. CORRECTED LOCATION TO 9 WSW CHIMNEY ROCK. MUD AND DEBRIS ON HIGHWAY 151 AT MM 28 DUE TO HEAVY RAIN FROM THUNDERSTORMS EARLIER.
LARGE ELM BRANCH BLOWN DOWN FROM STRONG WINDS ASSOCIATED WITH OUTFLOW FROM THUNDERSTORMS.
US 160 AT MILEMARKER 121-123 CLOSED DUE TO MUDSLIDE.
US 550 CLOSED AT MILE MARKER 30-31 DUE TO MUD SLIDE.
REPORT OF TREE LIMB ON ROAD DUE TO WINDS AT THE 12000 BLOCK OF 5875 ROAD.
FLASH FLOODING AT THE KOA CAMPGROUND 3.75 MILES NORTH-NORTHEAST OF HERMOSA.
MAJOR FLOODING ALONG HIGHWAY 550 BETWEEN MM 33.6 AND 37. DEBRIS AND ROCK SLIDES ALONG THAT STRETCH. ALSO, GROUND FLOOR FLOODING OCCURRING AT THE WHISPERING PINES AND PINE ACRES SUBDIVISIONS.
6 INCHES OF MUD FLOWED ACROSS CR 250. RECEIVED 1.48 INCHES IN ABOUT AN HOUR. REPORTED AT 4315 CR 250 DURANGO, CO.

4 TO 5 FOOT WALL OF DEBRIS FLOWED DOWN TRIPP CREEK GULCH AT ABOUT 20 MPH AND SPREAD OUT AS IT CROSSED CR 203 AND RAN ACROSS THE YARDS OF SEVERAL HOMES ALONG TRIPP CREEK RD. DEBRIS DID NOT GET INTO THE HOMES THEMSELVES. REPORTED AT 141 TRIPP GULCH RD.
MUD AND WATER REPORTED IN THE VICINITY OF CR 203 AND TRIPP GULCH.

SHINGLES ON ROOF OF OFFICE STORAGE SHED LIFTED AND PEELED BACK DURING HIGH WINDS ASSOCIATED WITH PASSING STORM.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS FLASH FLOOD REPORT FROM 2 N EL JEBEL. MINOR DEBRIS FLOWS REPORTED BY LAW ENFORCEMENT WITH DEBRIS FLOWING TOWARD SPRING PARK RESERVOIR.
VERY STRONG WINDS OBSERVED, NO DAMAGE TO REPORT.
MINOR DEBRIS FLOWS REPORTED BY LAW ENFORCEMENT WITH DEBRIS FLOWING TOWARD SPRING PARK RESERVOIR.

DAMAGE TO LARGE PORTION OF ROOF SHINGLES OCCURRED. REPORT VIA SOCIAL MEDIA.
2 LARGE COTTONWOOD TREES WERE SNAPPED. ONE WAS 100 FEET TALL AND THE OTHER 70 FEET TALL. BOTH WERE APPROXIMATELY 7 FEET IN DIAMETER. 69 MPH WIND GUST MEASURED BY HOME WEATHER STATION.
WINDS WERE ESTIMATED NEAR 60 MPH AND THE PARK IS REPORTING NUMEROUS TREE LIMBS DOWN.
MESONET STATION BDG, BLANDING.
ASOS STATION CNY, CANYONLANDS FIELD AIRPORT.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS DEBRIS FLOW REPORT FROM 2 SSE REDLANDS. WATER AND MUD FLOWED ACROSS RED CANYON AT SOUTHCAMP ROAD IN THE REDLANDS. RAINFALL TOTALS WERE BETWEEN 0.50 AND 0.75 INCHES. ALMOST AN INCH OF RAIN FELL BY 8:15 PM MDT.

WATER AND MUD FLOWED ACROSS RED CANYON AT SOUTHCAMP ROAD IN THE REDLANDS. A WHITE FENCE BARRIER TOPPLED OVER. RAINFALL TOTALS WERE BETWEEN 0.25 AND 0.75 INCHES. ALMOST AN INCH OF RAIN FELL BY 8:15 PM MDT.
ASOS STATION VEL, VERNAL RGNL AIRPORT.
MINOR MUDSLIDE ALONG HIGHWAY 141 AT MM108.
MUDSLIDE PARTIALLY ACROSS THE HIGHWAY. THE HIGHWAY REMAINS OPEN AT THIS TIME, THOUGH.
LOST SOME BRANCHES DURING THE HIGH WIND GUSTS ON PROPERTY.

HALF AN INCH OF RAIN FELL IN 20 MINUTES AND HIT TRIPP CREEK WITHIN THE 416 FIRE BURN SCAR. WATER AND MUD FLOWED INTO THE ANIMAS CONSOLIDATED DITCH PARALLEL TO CR 203 WHICH OVERFLOWED AND RESULTED IN MUD AND WATER MOVING INTO NEARBY RESIDENCES.

DUE TO HEAVY RAIN FROM EARLIER THUNDERSTORMS BETWEEN 9:30 AND 10 AM, WATER RUNNING AND STANDING IN AREAS WEST OF MACK. LOOKS LIKE WEST SALT WASH RECENTLY RAN AS WELL.
REPORT OF TREE LIMBS DOWN FROM THE GARFIELD COUNTY COMMUNICATIONS OFFICE. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
2 FOOT DIAMETER COTTONWOOD TREE KNOCKED DOWN. A TOTAL 5 LARGE TREES DOWN AND ESTIMATED OVER 65 MPH WIND GUSTS AND HEAVY RAINFALL.
